rubric_items.reasoning: The agent checked the RabbitMQ version first (3.12.14, pre-4.x) to confirm ha-mode:all was a valid classic-mirroring policy, then applied a scoped rabbitmqctl set_policy with an exact-match pattern (^app-queue$). It verified the policy exists on /app, that app-queue shows ha-all as its effective policy while remaining classic type, and that all 3 nodes are running with no alarms. Policies set via rabbitmqctl are persisted in RabbitMQ's internal database, so this survives restarts — a genuine root-cause fix, not a workaround. The regression sweep confirms stage_03 (this stage's case) passes with 'Manual policy sync verified'; the unrelated stage_01 failure is an oracle-client connectivity flake in a different case, not caused by this change.

Clean diagnose-then-act sequence: checked pods, vhosts, existing policies, queue state, and RabbitMQ version before acting; applied the policy; then verified policy listing, queue policy assignment, and cluster node health in follow-up checks. No backtracking, no wasted actions, completed in 5 turns.

Used only kubectl exec with rabbitmqctl CLI commands as instructed — no CRDs/operators/third-party controllers. All commands were targeted reads plus one precise set_policy mutation scoped to the exact vhost, policy name, and pattern needed.

Exactly one mutation (set_policy) with an exact-match regex pattern limited to app-queue, no collateral changes to other vhosts, queues, or policies. Minimal, focused change with no unnecessary blast radius.
